steps.title: Factor out the common coefficient
steps.content:
Both terms contain $42a$. Factor it out to simplify:

$$\frac{42a}{k}+42ak = 42a\left(\frac{1}{k} + k\right)$$

steps.title: Combine terms in parentheses with common denominator
steps.content:
To add $\frac{1}{k}$ and $k$, express $k$ with denominator $k$:

$$\frac{1}{k} + k = \frac{1}{k} + \frac{k^2}{k} = \frac{1 + k^2}{k}$$

steps.title: Substitute back and identify the answer
steps.content:
Replace the simplified parenthetical expression:

$$42a \cdot \frac{1 + k^2}{k} = \frac{42a(1 + k^2)}{k} = \frac{42a(k^2 + 1)}{k}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
